howItWorks.intro: This electricity bill calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Estimated kWh = Square Footage × Regional Factor
howItWorks.outro: Energy costs and incentives change by region and season. Check with your local utility for current rates.

faq.question: What is the average electric bill?
faq.answer: US average is about $125-150/month (~900 kWh). Southern states average $150-170 (AC costs). Northeast: $120-140. Pacific states: $100-120. Bills vary widely by home size, efficiency, and local rates.

faq.question: Why is my electric bill so high?
faq.answer: Common culprits: old HVAC system, poor insulation, electric water heater, space heaters, pool pump, old refrigerator, or phantom loads. Get a home energy audit to identify the biggest opportunities.

faq.question: How can I lower my electric bill?
faq.answer: Top 5 actions: 1) Smart thermostat (save 10-15%), 2) LED bulbs everywhere, 3) Seal air leaks, 4) Energy Star appliances, 5) Lower water heater to 120°F. Combined, these can cut bills 20-30%.
